title: artemisia eriantha subsp. altopyrenaica
intro: Artemisia eriantha subsp. altopyrenaica is a subspecies of the shrubby perennial plant Artemisia eriantha, native to the mountainous regions of northern Greece. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, such as digestive problems, fever, and respiratory infections, due to its high content of sesquiterpene lactones, which have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Artemisia eriantha subsp. altopyrenaica can be prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, making it a popular ingredient in traditional medicine and natural health remedies.
